title: /ai/work Evidence Surface
summary: The derived artifact registry: ten real cards from one build-time array, honest access labels, sensor-backed verified stamps — replacing the two-project accordion with dead Live badges.

strategy.decision: Render the whole evidence surface from ONE derived array (GitHub API at build time), with per-card access labels (live/install/clone/read/gated) and mono-face verified stamps as the signature visual element.
strategy.why: The current build page shows 2 projects (one dead, one mislabeled) while ~12 live artifacts exist unreferenced (goal doc § Missing; P5). The evaluating client's JTBD is claim-verification in minutes — every card must resolve to a loading artifact (`research/personas/evaluating-client.md`). Access labels never overpromise: Ask BC renders 'gated', Atelier 'live' per probe reality (`research/current-state/evidence-base-public.md`). Stamps make the freshness architecture legible (03-design-brief.md §4).
strategy.shipped: Two-project accordion: Ask Dad badged Live (500s in use, stack tag claims Vercel on a Cloudflare site) and Code to Cognition badged Live (links a 404 route; real home is behind an expired cert).
strategy.gap: Hand-maintained accordion of 2 (both broken) → derived registry of 10 (all probed), with track chips linking evidence back into paths.
strategy.question: Do access labels read instantly to a non-engineer evaluator, or do 'clone'/'gated' need a hover gloss? Check in stakeholder review.

currentState.summary: Two hardcoded project cards, both badged Live, both failing their claims; none of the real artifact set appears.
currentState.annotation: Proposed is data-derived end to end: a repo push updates a card on the next scheduled build; a failed liveness probe fails the build before a visitor meets the dead link.
